blockers: Reduce novelty from 0.74 to about 0.52. The cross-tradition architecture claim is a meaningful extension, but the minimum-self prerequisite, functional self, no-self continuity problem, and healthy ego before no-self are strong prior art.

closest_prior_art.difference: Engler's frame is Buddhist psychology and psychotherapy. He does not build a comparative typology of causal continuity, ontological identity, temporal collapse, and divine...
closest_prior_art.novelty_impact: High reduction. The core minimum-self prerequisite is already a well-known practitioner-facing thesis.
closest_prior_art.overlap: Very close. Engler says meditation requires ego strength, self-observation, and affect tolerance before no-self can be safely practiced.
closest_prior_art.source: Jack Engler interview, You Must Be Somebody Before You Can Be Nobody, Inquiring Mind, https://inquiringmind.com/article/0501_1_engler-interview/

closest_prior_art.difference: This is an intra-Buddhist metaphysical and doctrinal dispute, not a general model of practice architecture across traditions.
closest_prior_art.novelty_impact: High reduction. The pressure between no-self and soteriological functioning is ancient, not newly discovered.
closest_prior_art.overlap: Very close historical prior art. Pudgalavadins argued that karma, rebirth, liberation, benevolence, and lived personhood strain a strict no-self aggregate account.
closest_prior_art.source: Pudgalavada Buddhist Philosophy, Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://iep.utm.edu/pudgalavada-buddhist-philosophy/

closest_prior_art.difference: The focus is Theravada discourse and imagery, not cross-tradition engineering constraints.
closest_prior_art.novelty_impact: High reduction for the Buddhist side. The Lumenary idea extends rather than originates the continuity problem.
closest_prior_art.overlap: Collins directly studies how Theravada constructs personal identity and continuity on the apparently impossible basis of denying self.
closest_prior_art.source: Steven Collins, Selfless Persons, summary and citation, https://buddhanature.tsadra.org/index.php/Books/Selfless_Persons

frontiers.core_claim: Every contemplative tradition faces a paradox it cannot avoid: if practice causes awakening, the awakening depends on something temporary; if awakening was always the case, practice seems unnecessary. The resolution of this paradox is not a peripheral doctrinal detail. frontiers.core_claim: Identity-reframing contemplative practices differ in where they locate the continuity that lets a practitioner receive, survive, interpret, and integrate transformation. Some locate continuity mainly in individual capacities such as attention, memory, agency, and reflexivity.
